data.title: Arweave-Based AO Network Launches Staking Alpha Test to Enhance Decentralization
data.description:
On April 16, AO, a scalable blockchain network based on Arweave, announced the launch of its 'Network Availability Staking Alpha (NASA)' test program. According to BlockBeats, this initiative marks a significant step in the AO ecosystem, aiming to improve the availability and reliability of decentralized data networks through a staking mechanism. Currently in its Alpha phase, users can participate in network availability verification and earn rewards by providing data services to Arweave gateways and staking AO tokens.

In the initial trial, AO introduced an 'availability staking' mechanism, requiring node operators to stake 25 AO tokens to participate in the network and compete in terms of speed and stability in responding to user requests. The system allocates rewards from a monthly pool of 1,000 AO tokens based on nodes' performance in data services. This mechanism leverages the next-generation HyperBEAM architecture, enabling higher verifiability and trustlessness for gateway and routing services while significantly reducing operational costs.

The project team stated that NASA aims to establish a stronger decentralized economic model for the entire permanent network infrastructure, paving the way for future expansion into areas such as computational scheduling, data indexing, and network services. Although the current reward scale is small and still in the testing phase, the program is seen as a crucial starting point for the AO-Core economic system and will gradually expand to more network infrastructure services.

data.title: Thailand's GDP Growth Forecast Lowered Amid Middle East Conflict Impact
data.description: Thailand's central bank has reported a slowdown in the country's GDP growth during the first quarter, attributing the deceleration to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. According to Jin10, the central bank anticipates that the GDP growth rate may further decline in the second quarter. Consequently, the bank has revised its baseline GDP growth forecast for 2026 down to 1.3%, a decrease from the 1.9% projection made in December of the previous year.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Spot Silver Rises 1% to $79.75 per Ounce
data.description: Spot silver has seen its daily gains expand to 1.00%, currently trading at $79.75 per ounce. According to Jin10, this increase reflects ongoing market dynamics influencing precious metals prices.

data.title: Trump issues permit to facilitate oil transport between the US and Canada (Jinshi Data APP)
data.description: Documents released by the White House show that US President Trump issued several pipeline permits on Wednesday, including one for a new pipeline designed to facilitate the transport of crude oil and petroleum products between the United States and Canada. The permit was awarded to Bakken Pipeline for the construction of pipeline facilities in Burke County, North Dakota. He also issued other permits for the maintenance and operation of existing pipelines in North Dakota and Michigan, near the border. (Jinshi)

data.title: Foreign Investment in Japanese Stocks Reaches ¥39.433 Trillion as of April 10
data.description: Foreign investors purchased Japanese stocks worth ¥39.433 trillion as of April 10, according to Jin10. The previous value was revised from ¥29.596 trillion to ¥29.518 trillion.

data.title: STOCKS | CITIC Securities Highlights Strong Performance in Auto Exports and AI Developments
data.description: CITIC Securities has noted that March's production and sales data from major automakers have exceeded expectations, particularly in exports and high-end vehicle sales. According to Jin10, the firm emphasized that the export of new energy passenger vehicles is becoming a structural strength due to high oil prices and geopolitical conflicts overseas. The market is gradually becoming desensitized to high oil prices and war factors, and CITIC Securities suggests actively seizing opportunities in high-growth sectors. The ongoing positive impact of AI on the internal combustion engine market, the approval of Full Self-Driving (FSD) in the Netherlands, and the high growth in the robotics sector are driving continuous industry evolution. Currently, the stock prices of leading companies have stabilized, and CITIC Securities recommends actively positioning for the second quarter's new catalysts.

data.title: High-Stakes NBA Play-In Game Draws Significant Bet
data.description:
A significant bet has been placed on the NBA play-in game between the Orlando Magic and the Philadelphia 76ers. According to Odaily, an account with over $2 million in profits has invested $490,000 on the Magic to win, with an average opening price of 49.9 cents.

The game, which began at 7:30 AM UTC+8, will determine which team faces the Boston Celtics in the first round of the playoffs. The losing team will compete against the Charlotte Hornets for the final Eastern Conference playoff spot.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Mythos Model by Anthropic Shows Significant Advancements, Citic Securities Reports
data.description: Citic Securities has highlighted that Mythos, Anthropic's latest model, demonstrates significant advancements in capabilities, surpassing previous iterations in areas such as coding, reasoning, and research assistance. According to Jin10, the model also accelerates research and development processes in industries like biochemical research, finance, AI training, and robotics. Despite its superior alignment, the model's powerful capabilities pose a greater risk of infrequent errors, leading to its availability being restricted to core clients only. Anthropic is backed by investments and computational support from Amazon and Google, with recent collaborations strengthening their partnership. Starting in 2027, the demand for Google's TPU is expected to reach 3.5GW, with the Google TPU supply chain likely to benefit from the expanding demand for training cutting-edge models.

data.title: Account with over $2 million in profit and a total purchase of $490,000; Magic defeat 76ers in NBA play-in tournament.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, an account with over $2 million in profits (address: 0xbddf61af533ff524d27154e589d2d7a81510c684) purchased $490,000 worth of bets on the Magic defeating the 76ers in the Polymarket "NBA Play-In Game Magic vs. 76ers" prediction event, with an average opening price of $49.96. The NBA Play-In Game Magic vs. 76ers game started at 7:30 AM Beijing time today. The winner of this game will face the Celtics in the first round of the playoffs, while the loser will compete against the Hornets for the last playoff spot in the Eastern Conference. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, seeing changes before prices are set.

data.title: Japanese Companies Oppose Rate Hikes Amid Iran War Concerns, Survey Finds
data.description: A recent Reuters survey reveals that the proportion of Japanese companies opposing interest rate hikes has nearly doubled due to concerns over the war in Iran. According to Jin10, 42% of Japanese firms believe that profit growth is possible if the average price of WTI crude oil remains below $100 per barrel, while 28% set this threshold at $80. Most Japanese companies are either experiencing or anticipating the impact of the Iran war on their business operations.

data.title: Expert Predicts Bitcoin Bottom in October 2026 Based on Cycle Timing
data.description: Benjamin Cowen has projected that Bitcoin may reach its bottom in October 2026, aligning with historical cycle patterns. According to NS3.AI, Cowen suggests that this timing is approximately a year after Bitcoin's cycle peak. He also mentioned that a bottom in May could occur, but only if Bitcoin experiences a significant capitulation event that surpasses typical midterm year trends.

data.title: Nasdaq Rises for 11th Straight Session Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description:
The Nasdaq Composite Index extended its gains for the 11th consecutive session, reflecting investor optimism despite ongoing tensions in the Middle East. According to CoinDesk, the market's upward trajectory suggests that investors are focusing on other factors beyond geopolitical conflicts. This sustained rally highlights a broader confidence in the market's resilience and potential for growth.

Market analysts suggest that the continued rise of the Nasdaq is driven by strong performances in the technology sector, which has shown robust growth and innovation. The tech industry's ability to adapt and thrive in challenging environments has been a key factor in maintaining investor confidence. Additionally, economic indicators and corporate earnings reports have contributed to the positive sentiment, overshadowing concerns about geopolitical instability.

While the situation in the Middle East remains a point of concern, investors appear to be prioritizing domestic economic factors and corporate performance. The Nasdaq's performance is seen as a reflection of the market's ability to navigate complex global issues while maintaining a focus on long-term growth opportunities. As the situation evolves, market participants will continue to monitor developments closely, balancing geopolitical risks with economic prospects.

data.title: Ether's Institutional Accumulation and Market Dynamics Amidst DApp Challenges
data.description:
Ether (ETH) has shown resilience by maintaining a price above $2,300, distancing itself from the March 29 low of $1,940. According to Cointelegraph, this recent rally has led to an increase in ETH futures open interest, reaching $25.4 billion, signaling a rise in demand for leveraged positions. This movement suggests a potential shift in momentum for ETH bulls after several weeks of unsuccessful attempts to reclaim the $2,400 level.

Despite the positive price movement, the ETH perpetual futures funding rate has struggled to remain above 5% since Friday, indicating a lack of confidence among bulls. The metric has dipped below 0% multiple times, reflecting an excess demand for bearish leveraged positions. Under neutral conditions, the indicator should range between 5% and 10% to compensate for the cost of capital. Nevertheless, the recent rally to $2,350 appears to be supported by spot demand, as evidenced by US-listed Ether spot exchange-traded funds (ETFs) accumulating $248 million in net inflows over the past 10 days. Additionally, Bitmine Immersion announced the acquisition of $312 million worth of ETH, now holding 4.87 million ETH.

However, Bitmine’s ETH holdings are trading 13% below their acquisition cost, and US-listed Ether ETF assets under management have decreased to $13.7 billion from $20.5 billion three months ago. Ether's inability to reclaim the $2,400 mark coincides with the S&P 500 index reaching a new all-time high. Part of the reduced investor appetite for cryptocurrencies can be attributed to declining activity in decentralized applications (DApps). The 2026 bear market has negatively impacted various sectors, including memecoin token launch platforms, synthetic derivatives trading, and decentralized exchanges.

Ethereum's weekly DApps revenue has dropped to $11 million per week, down from $24 million in early February. Investors are questioning whether ETH is well-positioned to capture future demand for DApps, given the rise of competing blockchains like Hyperliquid and Plasma. Despite increased demand for ETH futures, derivatives metrics have not turned bullish. Contributing factors include losses in Ethereum strategic reserve companies and heightened competition in the DApps industry. The primary reason for accumulating ETH remains the expectation of higher on-chain processing demand and the burn mechanism, which incentivizes long-term holding.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | SPDR Gold Trust Holdings Increase by 2.285 Tons
data.description: The SPDR Gold Trust, the world's largest gold exchange-traded fund (ETF), has reported an increase in its holdings. According to Jin10, the fund's holdings rose by 2.285 tons, bringing the current total to 1,051.763 tons. This increase reflects ongoing interest and investment in gold as a precious metal.

data.title: Kevin Warsh's Upcoming Hearing for Federal Reserve Chair Role Scheduled for 21st
data.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessent announced that Kevin Warsh is set to face a hearing on the 21st of this month, potentially paving the way for him to assume the role of Federal Reserve Chair as planned. According to NS3.AI, Bessent praised Warsh as an outstanding candidate for the position. She also noted that Republican members of the Senate Finance Committee are in agreement with this assessment.

data.title: Fed Official Suggests Prolonged Rate Stability Amid Inflation Concerns
data.description: Federal Reserve official Moussalem has indicated that interest rates might need to remain within the current range of 3.50% to 3.75% for an extended period. According to NS3.AI, this stance is due to elevated oil prices, which could maintain core inflation around 3% by the end of the year. Moussalem also highlighted potential risks of rising inflation and projected that economic growth could decelerate to between 1.5% and 2% this year.

data.title: Tether-Backed SDEV Completes $134 Million Private Placement to Boost SKY Holdings
data.description: Stablecoin Development Corporation (SDEV), backed by Tether, has successfully completed a $134 million private placement. According to NS3.AI, the funds raised are primarily aimed at increasing SDEV's holdings in SKY tokens. Currently, SDEV possesses approximately 2.15 billion SKY tokens, which accounts for about 9.15% of the total supply.

data.title: Iran Considers Allowing Free Passage in Strait of Hormuz Amid Negotiations
data.description: Iran is contemplating permitting ships to navigate freely on the Omani side of the Strait of Hormuz, provided an agreement is reached to prevent further conflicts. According to Odaily, a source familiar with Tehran's stance revealed that this proposal is part of Iran's negotiation strategy. Iran intends to maintain control over its waters in the Strait, while Oman will manage its side independently. The source did not clarify whether Iran would agree to remove any mines it might have placed in the area or if all vessels, including those linked to Israel, would be granted free passage. The proposal's success hinges on Washington's willingness to meet Tehran's demands, which is crucial for any progress on the Strait of Hormuz issue.

data.title: Tron Proposal Sparks Controversy Over Token Lockups and Voting Rights
data.description:
Tron's latest proposal has stirred debate within the cryptocurrency community, as it suggests measures that could impact dissenters significantly. According to CoinDesk, the Tron founder has stated that those who oppose the proposal may face token lockups and be excluded from voting processes. This has raised concerns among participants who fear that such actions could undermine the democratic principles of the blockchain ecosystem.

The project, however, defends the proposal, arguing that it is designed to align all participants for the long-term success of Tron. The intention is to create a cohesive environment where stakeholders are incentivized to work towards common goals. Despite these assurances, the proposal has sparked a heated discussion about governance and the balance between authority and community input in blockchain projects. As the debate continues, the Tron community is closely watching how these proposed changes will unfold and what implications they might have for the future of the network.

data.title: Expert Highlights Tax Implications for Bitcoin Transactions
data.description: Nicholas Anthony, a research fellow at the Cato Institute, has highlighted the complexities of U.S. capital gains rules for Bitcoin users. According to NS3.AI, Anthony explained that individuals who make daily purchases with Bitcoin, such as buying coffee, could end up filing over 100 pages of tax paperwork by the end of the year. Each Bitcoin transaction must be reported on IRS Form 8949 and Schedule D. Anthony also noted the Virtual Currency Tax Fairness Act's proposed $200 exemption as a potential solution to simplify the reporting process.
